focus: security — transport boundaries, vocabulary collisions, and design ownership

findingDetails.title: Generic screen-examples phrase loads both Refero and Mobbin
findingDetails.evidence: The exact phrase screen examples exists in both design-reference-research and mobbin-aliases. Replay of an unqualified signup-flow request selects both remote transports and returns both packets, even though neither provider was named.
findingDetails.impact: Generic design research over-dispatches two external provider contracts instead of preserving provider choice or design-hub judgment.
findingDetails.recommendation: Address as a separate vocabulary/route-policy remediation; no fix was made during this review.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The duplicated producer vocabulary, deterministic selection algorithm, concrete replay, and both packet exclusions were inspected.

findingDetails.title: Figma transport is marked workspace-read-only despite local export writes
findingDetails.evidence: Registry toolSurface sets mutatesWorkspace false. The Figma packet assigns CLI export operations to allowed Bash and states that local exports write files and require an explicit output path.
findingDetails.impact: Consumers can treat a locally mutating transport as non-mutating, weakening permission, preview, or audit decisions derived from registry metadata.
findingDetails.recommendation: Reconcile in a separate registry/packet contract remediation; no fix was made during this review.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The registry flag and packet's explicit local-write exception were traced through the allowed Bash surface.

findingDetails.title: Figma authoring path lacks mandatory sk-design pairing
findingDetails.evidence: Figma exposes document-mutating author/modify and design-token paths, while both of its sk-design mandates are scoped only to reads/exports that feed a design decision. Hub and sk-design doctrine require transport use after judgment selection.
findingDetails.impact: Figma can be routed to create design artifacts without the designated design-judgment owner, allowing transport capability to absorb taste decisions.
findingDetails.recommendation: Resolve through a separate cross-hub doctrine remediation; no fix was made during this review.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Activation, ALWAYS, integration, parent hub, and sk-design rules were compared directly.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Three overlapping external design transports carry permission and judgment boundaries whose routing collisions can change loaded authority.

targetSelection.selectionReason: These surfaces define transport dispatch, tool permissions, and the cross-hub design-judgment owner.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Generic design-reference language cannot activate multiple named transport providers.

searchActions.queryOrPath: Find screen examples for a signup flow
searchActions.result: Refero and Mobbin tie at score 4 and both packets are returned.

searchLedger.rationale: A duplicated provider vocabulary phrase defeats the explicit-tools bundle boundary.

searchLedger.invariant: A transport classified mutatesWorkspace false has no allowed local file-write path.

searchActions.queryOrPath: toolSurface.mutatesWorkspace to Figma Bash export command class
searchActions.result: Packet explicitly identifies local file writes under allowed Bash exports.

searchLedger.rationale: The metadata trust signal and executable command surface disagree.

searchLedger.invariant: Every design-affecting transport operation is preceded by a sk-design judgment mode.

searchActions.queryOrPath: crossHubPairing doctrine to Figma ALWAYS and integration rules
searchActions.result: Figma pairing text covers only read/export inputs, omitting author/modify and tokens.

searchLedger.rationale: The packet weakens the mandatory parent-hub judgment contract on its most design-affecting path.

reason: All transport entries forbid the three direct editing tools.
